steps.title: Compare the two claims
steps.content: Text 1 claims emotional contagion requires physical interaction and body language. Text 2 reports evidence of emotional contagion through text-based social media posts, without in-person interaction. That directly challenges the necessity claim rather than contradicting the phenomenon itself.

steps.title: State the likely response
steps.content: Choice A focuses on smiling, which Text 2 does not address. Choice C says nonphysical studies were unclear, but Text 2 found evidence. Choice D wrongly limits the effect to negative emotions. Text 2 would say the underlined claim is incomplete because emotional contagion can occur without in-person interaction. The answer is B.
